title: VIDA 1.0 direct binary release
description: Canonical root epic for delivering VIDA 1.0 through bounded implementation waves from Binary Foundation to Primary Switchover.

title: Wave 1 - Binary Foundation
description: Rust workspace, crate boundaries, minimal bootable vida binary, thin command shell, fast local proof loop, and temp-state harness seam.

title: Wave 1.1 - Minimal Rust workspace and bootable vida scaffold
description: Bring the staged Binary Foundation scaffold into the repository root as a Rust workspace with a bootable vida binary and fail-closed root command shell.
close_reason: Binary Foundation initial scaffold, proof loop, and fail-closed hardening are complete

title: Wave 1.2 - Cheap local proof loop for Binary Foundation
description: Establish and prove canonical local build/test/help commands for Binary Foundation via Makefile and staged proof path.
close_reason: Cheap local proof loop is captured and proven in tracked execution

title: Wave 1.3 - Fail-closed shell hardening
description: Cover unknown commands, bad boot arguments, and temp-state seam robustness without entering Wave 2 semantics.
close_reason: Fail-closed shell hardening is proven and reconciled in tracked execution

title: Wave 1.4 - Command shell diagnostics and fail-closed reporting polish
description: Polish root shell diagnostics, unknown command handling, and bounded operator-visible fail-closed messaging without entering Wave 2 semantics.
close_reason: Command-shell diagnostics and fail-closed reporting polish are proven in tracked execution

title: Wave 1.5 - Binary Foundation closure receipts and handoff readiness
description: Capture closure-ready proof, receipts, and handoff state for Binary Foundation so Wave 2 can start lawfully.
notes:
Wave 1 closure reconciliation: Wave 1.1-1.4 are already closed and collectively prove the Binary Foundation owns and delivers the Rust workspace, crate boundaries, bootable vida binary, thin command shell, temp-state harness seam, and cheap local proof loop. Closure-ready proof artifacts: Cargo workspace in root/Cargo.toml, vida crate in crates/vida, Makefile canonical proof loop targets, fail-closed CLI tests in crates/vida/tests/boot_smoke.rs, and successful cargo test proof after subsequent Wave 3 work. Handoff posture: Wave 2 may start lawfully because Wave 1 substrate is present, proven, and not blocked by unresolved Binary Foundation gaps; remaining work in Wave 1.5 is receipt/handoff bookkeeping only.

Closure-ready proof rerun for handoff: `make vida-build`, `make vida-test`, and `make vida-run-help` all pass against the current repo state. Handoff note: later Wave 3 operator-surface work extends the binary beyond the original Wave 1 non-goals, but it does not invalidate Binary Foundation closure; it strengthens the already-proven host substrate.
close_reason: Binary Foundation closure receipts and lawful Wave 2 handoff state are now captured and verified

title: Wave 2 - State And Migration Backbone
description: Authoritative state spine, compatibility classification, migration preflight/runner, bridge import-export, and migration tests.

title: Wave 2.1 - Minimal authoritative state spine
description: Create the minimal binary-owned authoritative state entities and storage path needed to replace ad hoc temp-only state.
notes: Wave 2.1 closure summary: minimal authoritative state spine is now materially present as a DB-owned `state_spine_manifest` in embedded SurrealDB. The slice intentionally stays below compatibility classification and migration execution. Implemented scope: seed/read minimal authoritative spine summary, expose it in boot/status/doctor, preserve first initialization timestamp across reopen, and fail closed on missing or malformed manifest state. Proof: cargo test passes with unit coverage for manifest idempotency across reopen and missing-manifest failure, plus smoke coverage through boot/status/doctor surfaces. Review: external catch-review found no remaining remediation blockers after hardening.
close_reason: Minimal authoritative state spine is implemented, hardened, and verified

title: Wave 2.2 - Fail-closed boot compatibility classification
description: Classify startup compatibility and blocked boot outcomes before migration runner execution.
notes: Wave 2.2 progress summary: fail-closed boot compatibility classification is implemented and enforced. `boot` now stops on non-compatible classification, `status` and `doctor` surface the latest compatibility state, and `autonomous-next-task.py` no longer returns an empty selection merely because todo ui-json steps are absent. Hardening added a unit test for the incompatible path when instruction runtime state is missing. A direct external smoke test for command-level incompatible boot was attempted but ran into SurrealKV file-lock contention after intentional state corruption; the product logic is still covered by unit-level classification tests, while the lock behavior should be treated as a separate integration-testing constraint rather than folded into Wave 2.2 runtime law.
close_reason: Fail-closed boot compatibility classification is implemented, enforced, and verified

title: Wave 2.3 - Migration preflight and runner
description: Implement migration preflight, runner wiring, and blocked outcomes for incompatible state.

title: Wave 3 - Instruction And Operator Surface Integration
description: Instruction loading/composition, precedence activation runtime, and minimal operator-visible surfaces for boot/status/doctor/memory.

title: Wave 3.1 - Instruction loading and composition runtime
description: Load instruction artifacts and compose active instruction surfaces with preserved precedence.
notes: Wave 3.1 closure summary: instruction loading/composition runtime is materially present. Implemented scope includes instruction source ingest, dependency edge materialization from required_follow_on metadata, sidecar-backed artifact projection, deterministic mandatory-chain composition with cycle/missing-target fail-closed behavior, persisted effective bundle receipts, and DB-owned active instruction root selection via instruction_runtime_state. Hardening also preserves existing active root/runtime mode across bundle reseed so current runtime selection is not overwritten by framework defaults. Proof: cargo test passes with unit coverage for dependency cycles, missing targets, projection correctness, active-root loading, and root preservation across reseed; operator surfaces boot/memory/status/doctor resolve through the runtime-selected root rather than a literal artifact id. External catch-review reported no remaining remediation blockers after the reseed-preservation fix.
close_reason: Instruction loading and composition runtime are implemented, hardened, and verified

title: Wave 3.2 - Precedence activation runtime
description: Implement precedence-aware instruction activation runtime tied to canonical overlays and framework law.

title: Wave 3.3 - Minimal operator-visible boot status doctor memory surfaces
description: Expose minimal operator-visible boot/status/doctor/memory surfaces with fail-closed reporting, read-only inspection semantics where applicable, and operator-facing render modes (plain/color/color_emoji) with clean fallback behavior.
close_reason: Minimal operator-visible memory/status/doctor surfaces now work read-only, fail-closed, and support plain/color/color_emoji render modes

title: Wave 4 - Task Execution On Selected Flows
description: First real vida task execution flows, route/receipt enforcement on selected flows, resumable run-path wiring, and selected-flow bridge replacement steps.

title: Wave 4.1 - First selected vida task execution flow
description: Implement the first real selected vida task flow on top of the instruction and state backbones.

title: Wave 4.2 - Route and receipt enforcement on selected flows
description: Apply route and receipt enforcement to selected executable flows with fail-closed posture.

title: Wave 4.3 - Resumable run-path wiring
description: Wire resumable run-path behavior so selected flows can recover state and continue lawfully.

title: Wave 6 - Primary Switchover
description: Measured cutover, rollback-safe switchover, and final promotion proofs for VIDA 1.0.

title: Framework execution efficiency and autonomous follow-through hardening

title: Define and implement tracked autonomous execution protocol improvements

title: Define permanent next-task boundary analysis/report/update behavior across sessions
description: Under epic vida-stack-2d9.8, make continuous autonomous execution always perform next-task boundary analysis after closing a complex task: study nearby specs/code, emit a brief non-gating implementation-plan report, and update or create dependent spec/task coverage before entering the next task. Wire the behavior into framework-owned protocols and root overlay schema/config so it persists across new sessions.
notes: mode=autonomous; reason=Initialized via framework-wave-start for dev-pack
close_reason: done

title: Reconcile delegated review receipts and closure next-step drift after autonomous boundary hardening

Update nearest protocols/tests/task coverage as needed.
notes: mode=autonomous; reason=Initialized via framework-wave-start for dev-pack
close_reason: done

title: Canonicalize tracked writer execution when eligible analysis/review lanes are unavailable
description: Under epic vida-stack-2d9.8, fix the framework gap surfaced by vida-stack-2d9.8.3 where tracked writer blocks can become route-blocked with no eligible analysis lane and no eligible verifier/coach, forcing bounded main-lane self-diagnosis remediation instead of a canonical tracked path. Define and implement lawful task/route behavior for this case, including nearest protocol/runtime/test coverage, so tracked framework work either has a proper override/escalation path or fails in a way that does not require unsynchronized writer execution.
notes: mode=autonomous; reason=Initialized via framework-wave-start for dev-pack
close_reason: Canonical execution-auth route/override behavior now covers explicit no_eligible_verifier and framework-tracked no_eligible_analysis_lane remediation without unsynchronized local writer fallback

This planning gate must record which current implementation files, commands, artifact shapes, and runtime behaviors were inspected to define parity expectations for each upcoming slice.

Before closure, capture the donor runtime entrypoints and current artifact surfaces that this skeleton is expected to replace or absorb later.
notes: Rust workspace skeleton, crate boundaries, base dependency graph, CI wiring, and thin runtime-family entry surfaces are now present in the active tree.

title: Bootstrap TaskFlow v1 test harness, golden corpus, and donor parity fixtures
description: Before implementing TaskFlow crates, build the test-first foundation: workspace test harness, crate templates with test scaffolds, JSONL/TOON golden corpus, donor parity fixtures from taskflow-v0 and monolithic vida, and failure-mode/recovery test shape for the runtime kernel.
notes: TaskFlow test-foundation progress is now green for golden corpus, failure-mode, and donor-parity slices around native dependency-planning surfaces: tests/golden/taskflow covers critical-path and broken-edge validate-graph outputs, the binary smoke suite proves fail-closed second-parent rejection, and donor semantic fixtures now cover default taskflow-v0 task ready, task show, and task list outputs under tests/golden/taskflow/donor_ready_semantic.json, donor_show_semantic.json, and donor_list_semantic.json. Both donor and Rust runtime surfaces match the bounded semantic cores for ready-set, task-show, and visible default task-list planning views despite intentional raw JSON shape differences. cargo test -p vida, cargo test -p taskflow-format-jsonl, cargo test -p taskflow-format-toon, python3 codex-v0/codex.py check --profile active-canon, and python3 codex-v0/codex.py proofcheck --profile active-canon-strict are green. The broader task remains in_progress because wider donor parity fixtures and recovery coverage are still incomplete.
close_reason: TaskFlow test foundation is now closure-ready: package-local JSONL/TOON golden harness is green, binary vida smoke covers native dependency-planning and fail-closed recovery paths, and donor semantic parity fixtures now cover default taskflow-v0 task ready, task show, and task list surfaces with active-canon check/proof green.

For each crate slice, inspect and record the current donor commands, source files, and artifact shapes being preserved or intentionally changed.

title: Implement state kernel with filesystem and SurrealDB adapters
description: Implement taskflow-state plus bounded filesystem and SurrealDB adapters, keeping storage law behind contracts while making embedded SurrealDB on kv-surrealkv the product storage line. Before each state slice closes, inspect and record the corresponding donor persistence behavior, JSONL/DB artifact shapes, and migration semantics from taskflow-v0 or monolithic vida.
notes: Extended state durability proofs to boot compatibility runtime state. After a compatible boot compatibility evaluation, reopening the same authoritative Surreal-backed store now has explicit proof that latest_boot_compatibility_summary preserves classification=compatible, empty reasons, and next_step=normal_boot_allowed. Synced docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md through condition 122 and revalidated with cargo test -p vida, finalize-edit, check --profile active-canon, and proofcheck --profile active-canon-strict.
close_reason: TaskFlow state kernel closure-ready: filesystem and SurrealDB adapters are implemented with canonical storage/spine/bootstrap contracts, authoritative snapshot export/import/replace bridge, reconciliation receipts and summaries, migration and boot runtime-state durability across reopen, and green cargo/doc proof cycle through docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md condition 122.

title: Implement flow, handoff, gates, and replay-safe recovery kernel
description: Implement the explicit transition engine, packet-driven handoff/context governance, coach-verifier-approval separation, and checkpoint/replay/recovery law required by Layers 5-7, with failure-mode, replay, and seam tests written and green for each new contract slice. Before each slice, inspect and record the current donor route/run-graph/gate behavior that defines preserved semantics.
notes: Aligned the launcher help contract after the run-graph in-process cutover: vida taskflow help run-graph now correctly describes run-graph as a launcher-owned in-process mutation and inspection surface instead of a delegated runtime path. Proven green via cargo test -p vida; docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md synced and active-canon check/proof cycle refreshed.
close_reason: Wave 3 flow kernel closure is now proven: authoritative Rust run-graph state persists across reopen, launcher-owned recovery/checkpoint/gate projections are green in JSON and text surfaces, direct per-run and latest flow inspections are green, run-graph init/update are in-process Rust mutations over VIDA_STATE_DIR, and launcher help/contracts are aligned with the in-process flow runtime.

title: TaskFlow v1 CLI and operator surface hardening
description: Harden the TaskFlow v1 CLI so it is installable on PATH, project-PWD driven, dependency-graph aware, self-diagnosing, and operationally discoverable without hidden env/bootstrap assumptions.
close_reason: All direct TaskFlow CLI hardening children are closed: bootstrap, deps/planning, help/query, and doctor surfaces are green, synced in docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md, and validated through active-canon check/proof cycles.

title: Define and implement canonical binary placement and project-PWD bootstrap contract
description: Ensure the final runtime ships one canonical binary path visible in PATH, launches from the project PWD without manual VIDA_ROOT exports, refuses ambiguous roots fail-closed, and removes hidden fallback dependence on nested .env resolution.
notes: Bootstrap root-discovery slice is green: vida taskflow now resolves the project root from nested project PWD ancestry when VIDA_ROOT is unset, exports the resolved root into the delegated runtime, and fails closed on ambiguous multi-root ancestry. cargo test -p vida is green, direct nested-PWD runtime proof is green, ambiguous-root refusal proof is green, and docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced.
close_reason: Closed after proving project-PWD root discovery without manual VIDA_ROOT, fail-closed ambiguous-root refusal, and installed vida binary placement that resolves sibling taskflow runtime binaries from the active bin root while exporting the delegated project root from cwd.

title: Implement native dependency graph commands and planning views
description: Add native task dependency mutation and inspection surfaces so backlog planning no longer requires export/import patching: dep add/remove, deps, reverse-deps, tree, ready --scope, blocked, critical-path, and graph validation for parent-child plus blocks edges.
notes: Native cli-deps parity is now green for deps, reverse-deps, blocked, tree, ready --scope, validate-graph, dep add/remove, and critical-path. The critical-path surface computes a deterministic longest unresolved blocks chain directly from the authoritative TaskFlow runtime store, while prompt-template proof drift is also resolved and docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced. Awaiting final active-canon check/proofcheck pass in this sync cycle before closing the task.
close_reason: Native cli-deps parity is green end-to-end: deps, reverse-deps, blocked, tree, ready --scope, validate-graph, dep add/remove, and critical-path now run in-process over the authoritative TaskFlow runtime store; cargo test -p vida, direct runtime proofs, active-canon check, and active-canon-strict proofcheck are green; docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced.

title: Redesign TaskFlow help surface with command contracts, examples, and failure modes
description: Replace the flat help surface with layered command help that exposes purpose, source-of-truth notes, dependency semantics, canonical examples, exit/failure modes, and operator recipes for the most common taskflow workflows.
close_reason: Closed after proving launcher-owned layered TaskFlow help with source-of-truth notes, dependency semantics, canonical examples, operator recipes, fail-closed guidance, topic help, and command-help routing in the vida binary.

title: Implement optimal operator query and answer surface for TaskFlow
description: Add a canonical query/help interaction for TaskFlow so operators can ask for the best next command or workflow query and receive the optimal bounded answer for the current runtime context. The slice must define the query contract, the answer shape, decision criteria, examples, and failure modes without hardcoded project-specific roles/models/tools. Before closure, inspect existing taskflow-v0 CLI/help behavior and preserve lawful command-home semantics.
close_reason: Closed after proving launcher-owned deterministic TaskFlow query answers with bounded intent mapping, recommended command output, failure-mode guidance, and query help inside the vida binary.

title: Implement CLI self-diagnosis for bootstrap, task store, and graph health
description: Add doctor surfaces that verify current binary path, resolved project root, workspace paths, task-store health, helper/runtime dependency health where applicable, orphan edges, invalid graph states, and migration/import-export parity so operators can diagnose the runtime quickly.
notes: Extended doctor diagnostics are green: vida doctor now verifies launcher/runtime path resolution and native dependency graph health in addition to storage/spine/task-store/run-graph/compatibility/migration/effective-bundle checks. cargo test -p vida and direct cargo run -p vida -- boot/doctor proof are green, docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced, and awaiting final active-canon check/proofcheck in this sync cycle before closing the task.
close_reason: Extended doctor diagnostics are green end-to-end: vida doctor now verifies launcher/runtime path resolution and native dependency graph health in addition to storage/spine/task-store/run-graph/compatibility/migration/effective-bundle checks; cargo test -p vida, direct boot/doctor runtime proof, active-canon check, and active-canon-strict proofcheck are green; docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced.

title: Execute final full-system test matrix and cutover rehearsal
description: Execute the explicit final system-wide matrix for the new runtime family: workspace-wide crate/unit/integration tests, TOON/JSONL golden tests, donor parity runs, recovery/replay tests, launcher/installer migration tests, taskflow-to-docflow end-to-end consumption tests, and cutover rehearsal evidence before promotion proofs and bridge retirement.
notes: The integrated boot_smoke, crate-wide `vida`, and workspace-wide cargo matrix tranches are now green; the live repo-root `taskflow -> docflow` consumption path is explicitly proven under a clean launcher environment; the current-tree donor-parity semantic fixtures for `ready`, `show`, and default `list` are re-proven through exact `task_smoke` parity tests; the launcher-owned recovery inspection surfaces for `latest`, `status`, `checkpoint`, and `gate` are re-proven through exact `boot_smoke` recovery CLI tests; the non-empty latest-flow run-graph and recovery bridge surfaces are now also re-proven through exact `boot_smoke` latest-state tests; the launcher/installer follow-through surfaces are re-proven through exact installed/archive `boot_smoke` tests; and the booted cutover-preflight surfaces are re-proven through exact status/doctor checks plus active-canon readiness-check and active-canon-strict proofcheck. The remaining gap has narrowed to broader promotion-proof closure under `vida-rf1.3.3`.
close_reason: Full-system matrix and cutover-rehearsal slices are now explicitly re-proven across workspace cargo, repo-root taskflow->docflow consumption, donor parity, recovery inspection, latest-flow bridge state, launcher/installer follow-through, and cutover-preflight readiness/proof surfaces; remaining work is promotion-proof closure owned by vida-rf1.3.3.

title: Retire bounded bridges and flip the primary runtime surfaces
description: Retire or bound the remaining taskflow-v0, codex-v0, and monolithic bridge paths once parity is proven and the new runtime-family surfaces are the canonical entry points.
notes: The current integrated tree now re-proves fourteen bounded bridge-retirement slices: the exact installed/project-aware sentinel smoke confirms the launcher-owned in-process TaskFlow `task` surfaces still stay off the delegated `taskflow-v0` binary in both nested project mode and installed mode, the exact installed-mode compatibility smokes confirm that installed `vida docflow` remains bounded to `overview` while rejecting non-`overview` commands, the exact `boot_smoke` overview-scan proof confirms that `vida docflow overview-scan` stays on the Rust/in-process side, the exact installed-mode `boot_smoke` proofs confirm that installed `vida` still resolves the primary TaskFlow path from its own `bin` directory and serves helper-backed `task ready --json` semantics with blocked-sibling filtering and deterministic ordering, the exact `boot_smoke` shell-entry proof confirms that `vida docflow` can still use the Rust CLI shell directly, the exact shell-contract proofs confirm that `vida docflow` help stays runtime-specific while unsupported commands fail closed instead of widening back into a retired bridge path, the exact `boot_smoke` readiness-check proof confirms that `vida docflow readiness-check` stays on the Rust/in-process side, the exact `boot_smoke` proofcheck proof confirms that `vida docflow proofcheck` stays on the Rust/in-process side, the exact `boot_smoke` doctor proof confirms that `vida docflow doctor` stays on the Rust/in-process side, the exact `boot_smoke` summary proof confirms that `vida docflow summary` stays on the Rust/in-process side, the exact `boot_smoke` protocol-coverage proof confirms that `vida docflow protocol-coverage-check` stays on the Rust/in-process side, the exact `boot_smoke` fastcheck proof confirms that `vida docflow fastcheck` stays on the Rust/in-process side, the exact `boot_smoke` activation-check proof confirms that `vida docflow activation-check` stays on the Rust/in-process side, and the exact `boot_smoke` layer-status proof confirms that `vida docflow layer-status` stays on the Rust/in-process side. Remaining scope stays at broader bridge retirement across the remaining bounded compatibility surfaces and primary-runtime flip.

title: Runtime-family delivery, installer, and unified vida CLI track
description: Deliver the new runtime family as installable user-facing system tooling: CI/CD, release builds, installer automation, must-have internet fetch/install behavior, and one unified vida CLI surface exposing taskflow and docflow as subcommands.
notes: Delivery track has started: the unified vida CLI surface is closed, while CI/CD, installer, fetch/bootstrap, and packaging waves remain open.

title: Implement full CI/CD for runtime-family builds, tests, proofs, and releases
description: Implement the full delivery pipeline for TaskFlow and DocFlow: workspace builds, unit/integration/e2e matrix, parity/proof jobs, release packaging, artifact publishing, and cutover-safe release gating for the sibling runtimes.

title: Implement canonical installer and system installation scripts for user machines
description: Extend the existing installer so it installs the runtime-family binaries and wrappers into the user system, makes the canonical binaries visible in PATH, configures the required launch surfaces, and validates the resulting install end-to-end from project PWD.

title: Implement must-have internet dependency/bootstrap fetches inside installer
description: During installer implementation, explicitly fetch and install all mandatory external prerequisites from the internet that are required for the user-facing CLI/runtime to work, verify their versions and integrity where possible, and fail closed with actionable diagnostics when a required dependency cannot be provisioned.

title: Implement unified vida CLI with `vida taskflow` and `vida docflow` surfaces
description: Implement the unified user-facing vida CLI that exposes the sibling runtimes as explicit subcommands, including at minimum `vida taskflow` and `vida docflow`, while preserving bounded runtime-family ownership and avoiding hidden command-home ambiguity.
notes: Unified vida CLI surface is closure-ready: root vida --help exposes explicit taskflow/docflow command homes, vida taskflow help is launcher-owned and bounded, vida docflow help shows the real in-process command map, and routing semantics are fail-closed rather than hidden. docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced; awaiting final active-canon check/proofcheck in this sync cycle before formal closure.
close_reason: Unified vida CLI surface is green end-to-end: root vida --help exposes explicit taskflow/docflow command homes, vida taskflow help is launcher-owned and bounded, vida docflow help shows the real in-process command map, routing is fail-closed, and docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced; active-canon check and active-canon-strict proofcheck are green.

title: Spec Task - DB-first runtime ownership with export-only file surfaces
description: Define that direct 1.0 runtime state is DB-owned in SurrealDB: workflow_runtime for epics/tasks/TODO/trace state, subagent_runtime for agent operational state, instruction/framework memory as DB slices, and project-memory as sync/export rather than framework boot-ingest substrate.

title: Spec Task - Unified export protocol for JSON data, docs, memory, and logs
description: Define canonical export surfaces from the DB-owned runtime for JSON data, documentation bundles, memory ledgers, diagnostics, logs, and parity/proof artifacts, including operator-facing rendering modes with plain/color/color_emoji output and clean fallback behavior.

title: Spec Task - Add VIDA storage spec for SurrealDB embedded backend and kv-surrealkv
description: Research epic: vida-2qe. Create the missing VIDA spec delta that explicitly decides whether embedded SurrealDB is adopted, and if adopted defines kv-surrealkv as the backend, decision boundaries, non-goals, migration impact, and proof expectations before implementation.
close_reason: Active specs now make embedded SurrealDB on kv-surrealkv the canonical storage direction

title: Spec Task - Spec-sync protocol for autonomous framework changes
description: Define the rule that when autonomous development changes framework behavior, the agent must check for nearby governing specs/protocols and add or update them in the matching context before considering the work complete.

title: Spec Task - Instruction diff format and projection merge rules
description: Define the canonical structured diff format for instruction sidecars, along with projection merge order, conflict handling, and rebind behavior across migrations.

title: Implementation Task - Instruction slice runtime schema and sidecar patch application
description: Implement the minimal instruction-memory runtime on embedded SurrealDB with version metadata, immutable bundled artifacts, sidecar patch records, anchor validation, and effective projection support.

title: Protocol Task - Repeated-error web lookup rule for autonomous debugging
description: Define that after a repeated technical error or uncertain external API/format, autonomous debugging must escalate to primary-source lookup instead of repeating local trial-and-error.

title: Implementation Task - Source-tree ingest and migration bootstrap for DB-owned slices
description: Implement boot-time ingest from Git-resident source trees into DB-owned project, instruction, and framework slices with version metadata, hash-based change detection, and ordered migrations.
close_reason: Runtime-owned boot ingest for instruction and framework memory trees is implemented and proven

title: Architecture Task - Git source trees for project, instruction, and framework data slices
description: Define the Git-resident source tree layout for the three durable data slices, including nested directories, markdown/file support, metadata, and boot-time ingest into the DB-owned runtime.

title: Wave 4.4 - Selected-flow bridge replacement steps
description: Replace remaining bridge paths on the selected executable flows once route/receipt enforcement and resumable runtime behavior are stable.

title: Wave 2.4 - Bridge import export path and migration tests
description: Add bridge import/export path and migration-focused tests once authoritative state and migration runner exist.

title: Wave 3.4 - Instruction and migration/operator failure reporting
description: Harden operator-visible reporting for instruction and migration state failures without entering Wave 4 task execution.

title: Wave 5 - Verification Visibility And Orchestration Hardening
description: Richer proofs, parity/conformance proving, trace/eval/operator visibility, and lawful adaptive orchestration payload integration.

title: Agent-system payload integration across Waves A-F
description: Slice and integrate the autonomous role profiles and adaptive orchestration implementation plan as part of VIDA 1.0, not as a separate product.

title: Implementation Task - Architecture-aware optimized binary build targets
description: Add deterministic build/distribution support for per-architecture optimized VIDA binaries (for example x86_64 and aarch64), with explicit target labeling and no hidden CPU-extension assumptions in generic artifacts.

title: Protocol Task - Library evaluation matrix and live-source selection rule
description: Define mandatory live-source library evaluation with alternatives matrix, freshness/liveness checks, and explicit winner selection before adopting third-party crates for core runtime behavior.

title: Agent-system Iteration 1 - Sources profiles taxonomy role contracts
description: Implement source registry/refresh, role profiles, taxonomy, and role contracts.

title: Protocol Task - User approval gate between autonomous tasks
description: Define a mode where before each next task the agent gathers specs and next-step context, performs meta-analysis, presents the proposed task for user approval outside TODO execution, then after approval updates specs and implements the task fully.

title: Research Epic - SurrealDB embedded storage fit and spec coverage
description: Track research and spec-coverage analysis for adopting SurrealDB embedded storage with kv-surrealkv in VIDA and beads-adjacent flows.

title: Agent-system Iteration 2 - Routing scoring adaptive scaling consensus packets handoffs verification
description: Implement routing, scoring, adaptive agent counts, consensus orchestration, task packets, handoffs, and verification burden.

title: Implementation Task - Effective instruction bundle service and ordered boot emission
description: Implement a system service that resolves the ordered effective instruction bundle for boot/runtime, emits mandatory instruction chain output in one pass, and records optional-condition activation separately.

title: Architecture Task - Define three-slice memory model for project memory, instruction memory, and sidecars
description: Define the canonical three-slice memory/storage model covering project memory, instruction-system memory, and instruction sidecars; include immutable framework-owned instruction bundles, migration/version checks, and sidecar override/replacement semantics.

title: Wave 5.3 - Trace eval and operator visibility surfaces
description: Add trace/eval/operator visibility surfaces that support proving and debugging without changing runtime law.

title: Fix silent diagnosis parent issue drift in overlay configuration
description: vida-silent-diagnosis capture attempts to attach to parent_issue mobile-1hv, but that issue does not exist in the current beads workspace, causing capture failure.
notes: mode=autonomous; reason=Initialized via framework-wave-start for dev-pack
close_reason: Silent diagnosis parent issue drift fixed and verified

title: Implementation Task - Sidecar-aware instruction query and projection service
description: Implement sidecar-aware instruction retrieval so mandatory instruction chains are resolved from immutable base artifacts plus applicable sidecars, with explicit activation order and fail-closed anchor validation.

title: Wave 5.4 - Lawful adaptive orchestration payload integration
description: Integrate adaptive orchestration payloads where lawfully enabled after verification and visibility surfaces exist.

title: Research Task - SurrealDB spec audit for epic vida-2qe
description: Epic: vida-2qe. Audit active VIDA 1.0 specs for explicit SurrealDB embedded storage decisions, kv-surrealkv backend selection, and missing spec deltas needed before implementation.

title: Spec Task - Memory diff format for project and framework slices
description: Define the canonical record-level diff format for project and framework memory updates, including append, supersede, merge, reclassify, and deactivation semantics.

title: Implementation Task - Instruction dependency resolver and mandatory chained auto-read
description: Implement instruction dependency resolution so a selected base instruction automatically loads its required follow-on instructions into one effective boot/runtime read flow, while optional instructions remain condition-triggered.

title: Agent-system Iteration 3 - Evals traces operator visibility pilot rollout
description: Implement evals, traces, operator visibility, pilot execution, and rollout surfaces for the agent-system payload.

title: Implement runtime orchestration, provider seams, observability, and thin CLI
description: Implement runtime execution services, provider-neutral transport seams, observability/readiness surfaces, and the thin taskflow CLI without hardcoding providers, models, roles, or project flows; this wave must absorb the explicit CLI hardening track for binary placement, project-PWD bootstrap, dependency graph commands, help surface, and self-diagnosis before bridge retirement. Before each runtime/operator slice, inspect and record the current donor command behavior, runtime outputs, and closure surfaces being matched or intentionally changed.
notes: Donor-aligned role_selection now ships from vida taskflow consume final. Fallback proof for probe closure stays on orchestrator with auto_no_keyword_match; scope proof for clarify spec scope resolves scope_discussion, business_analyst, and spec-pack. cargo test -p vida is green, live consume-final proofs are green, docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced, and codex check plus proofcheck are green.
close_reason: Donor-aligned role_selection is now emitted by vida taskflow consume final with proven fallback and scope-discussion paths, cargo test -p vida green, live runtime proofs green, and docs plus canonical validation synchronized.

title: Prove TaskFlow bridge parity and retire donor-owned behavior
description: Use taskflow-v0 and the monolithic vida line only for parity, proof, and migration bridging; close behavior gaps, record donor mapping, and prepare bridge retirement without adding new permanent logic there.
notes: Task bridge routing is now installed/project-aware instead of repo-source-coupled, unsupported taskflow/task passthrough is explicitly proven, release artifacts now package the required helper scripts, and bridge parity proof now explicitly covers the remaining in-process task commands: list --all --json, show, next-display-id, update, close, and export-jsonl all run through the local DB bridge in both project-aware and installed-mode execution without requiring the delegated runtime binary; targeted bridge tests are green, full cargo test -p vida is green, docs check is green, and active-canon-strict proofcheck is green.
close_reason: Installed/project-aware TaskFlow bridge parity is now proven for unsupported passthrough and all remaining in-process task command surfaces, with delegated runtime bypass validated in project-aware and installed-mode execution, full cargo test -p vida green, docs check green, active-canon-strict proofcheck green, and independent verifier closure approval.

title: Bring TaskFlow to a working state for the configured project agent system
description: Close the remaining runtime gaps so TaskFlow can execute with the overlay-configured native project agent system where protocol_activation.agent_system=true, including validated project role/profile/flow resolution, native internal_subagents inventory and routing, dispatch/verification behavior, and end-to-end proofs that the configured project agent system works without hardcoded legacy routing or provider assumptions.
notes: TaskFlow consume final now proves fail-closed registry/flow validation, boundary-aware lane keyword matching, configured native agent_system plus Codex multi-agent topology, installed helper-aware bridge packaging, focused negative consume-final proofs, release artifact inspection, and full cargo test -p vida; run-graph seed materializes initial native dispatch state into the authoritative Rust run-graph for scope_discussion, pbi_discussion, and default implementation paths; run-graph advance now provides launcher-owned post-seed transitions for both configured conversational modes, plus the implementation path through analysis -> coach -> review_ensemble with overlay-derived review_findings gating and persisted recovery/status summaries, all without manual meta_json, with targeted advance tests green, full cargo test -p vida green, docs check green, and active-canon-strict proofcheck green; independent closure verification of the next implementation transition beyond review_ensemble remains the current open boundary.
close_reason: Launcher-owned TaskFlow now proves configured native agent-system behavior across consume-final selection, run-graph seed, and run-graph advance for the default implementation path through review_ensemble plus both configured conversational modes, with full cargo test -p vida green, docs check green, active-canon-strict proofcheck green, and independent verifier closure approval.

title: Implement operator CLI, wrappers, and runtime-family surfaces
description: Implement the thin DocFlow CLI, operator views, launcher/wrapper migration shims, and runtime-family activation surfaces without hardcoded project-specific tools or subagents. Before closure, inspect and record the existing donor launcher, wrapper, and command-home behavior that must be preserved or intentionally replaced.
notes: Operator/runtime-family surface is now green for in-process vida docflow overview, summary, layer-status, scan, fastcheck, doctor, activation-check, protocol-coverage-check, proofcheck, validation, readiness, check-file, readiness-file, registry, registry-scan, registry-write, overview-scan, validate-tree, readiness-tree, readiness-check, readiness-write, relations-scan, links, deps-map, artifact-impact, and task-impact; canonical registry/readiness writes and read-only relation/detail tracing now run inside vida by default, while remaining donor-backed behavior is limited to mutation/cutover parity handled by the bridge wave.
close_reason: In-process vida docflow now owns the operator and read-only relation/runtime-family surface; remaining donor behavior is limited to bridge/cutover parity.

title: Prove DocFlow bridge parity and prepare cutover
description: Use codex-v0 only as the parity oracle and bounded bridge, close output/behavior gaps, and prepare cutover to docflow-rs without leaving permanent logic in Python.
notes: Bridge wave remains active. vida docflow readiness-check --profile active-canon, proofcheck --profile active-canon-strict, finalize-edit, touch, rename-artifact, init, and move now all run in-process. Profile proof totals match the donor surface; the mutation slice now covers bounded footer metadata updates, changelog append, artifact-id mutation, artifact bootstrap creation, and markdown/changelog relocation with quiet changed-file validation without falling through to codex-v0. docflow-cli and vida regression coverage were added for all seven paths, cargo test -p docflow-cli and cargo test -p vida are green, docs/process/vida1-development-conditions.md is synced, and codex check/proofcheck active-canon validation remains green. Remaining bridge-wave focus is wrapper retirement and final default-routing replacement, with any residual mutation/read-only parity handled only if those cutover steps still depend on them.
close_reason: Bridge wave closed: vida docflow now covers profile-heavy readiness/proof paths, mutation parity for finalize-edit/touch/rename-artifact/init/move/migrate-links, read-side parity for changelog/changelog-task/task-summary, runtime-root-safe link validation, Rust-first fail-closed launcher routing, and installed docflow-v0 wrapper cutover to vida docflow without donor fallback.

title: Migrate runtime-family maps, installers, launchers, and helper scripts
description: Promote taskflow-rs and docflow-rs into the root-map/runtime-family stack, update installers and helper scripts, and preserve explicit discoverability without hidden legacy routes.
notes: Launcher/install migration is now closure-ready across the runtime-family stack, installers, launchers, and helper scripts: installed wrappers expose `vida docflow` as the canonical launcher contract and release manifest entrypoint, installed `vida docflow` is explicitly bounded to `help|overview only`, repo/dev and installed modes are separated clearly in help/runtime-family metadata/release notes, installer `doctor` fails closed on missing installed TaskFlow helpers, installed `codex-v0` is demoted to migration-only compatibility, CI installed smoke asserts only the canonical installed launcher contract, and built-archive installed proofs now verify the helper-backed canonical `vida taskflow task ready --json` path including default Python resolution, helper-path tracing, non-empty ready-set semantics, dependency exclusion, and deterministic ordering without delegated `taskflow-v0` fallback.
close_reason: Runtime-family launcher/install migration is now explicitly scoped, helper-backed, and end-to-end proven across installer, release archive, installed smoke, and targeted launcher tests without hidden legacy routes.

title: Implement release packaging, binary layout, and install artifact contracts
description: Define and implement release artifact naming, binary layout, packaging metadata, upgrade/rollback posture, and install-time verification contracts so shipped artifacts are deterministic and machine-installable.

title: Spec Task - Sidecar authoring and consistent projection rules for instruction updates
description: Define how an LLM or operator authors instruction-sidecar updates: delete, replace, insert, replace-with-many, anchor validation, and deterministic merge into one consistent effective output.
